panels.description: Wide shot, Mr. Darcy in a heavy overcoat, walking with purpose through the misty, gaslit streets of 19th-century London. His expression is somber, reflective, and determined. High contrast lighting.

bubbles.text: Darcy resolved to find them... he blamed his own pride for letting Wickham's character remain hidden.

panels.description: Medium shot of Mr. Darcy standing at the threshold of a cluttered, dim parlor in Edward Street, confronting a stern-looking, middle-aged woman, Mrs. Younge. She looks defensive.

bubbles.text: Mrs. Younge, the former governess, knew Wickham's whereabouts. Darcy sought her out, though she was reluctant to speak.

panels.description: Medium shot: Darcy enters a small, poorly furnished room. Wickham is lounging on a chair, smirking, while Lydia stands defiantly in the background, looking petulant and stubborn.
Close-up of Lydia, arms crossed, chin tilted up with a reckless, youthful grin. She looks entirely unbothered by Darcy's presence.

bubbles.text: I don't need help! We'll be married eventually, it doesn't matter when.

panels.description: Dynamic medium shot: Darcy stands tall, looming over a seated, visibly agitated Wickham. Wickham holds his head in his hands, looking desperate as he confesses his mounting gambling debts.

bubbles.text: I have to resign my commission... my debts are impossible, Darcy. I had no intention of marriage.
Darcy realized the only path was to bribe the man into a marriage he never intended to honor.
